seo.title: Where To Stay in Trat & Koh Chang: Best Areas (2026)
seo.metaDescription: Find the best area to stay in Trat and Koh Chang. White Sand Beach for convenience, Lonely Beach for backpackers, Koh Kood for luxury. Honest neighborhood comparison with hotel picks.

intro: Where you stay in Trat province depends on what kind of trip you want. Koh Chang's west coast beaches offer the widest range — from backpacker bungalows to 5-star resorts. Trat Town is a practical overnight base with genuine Thai character. The outer islands of Koh Mak and Koh Kood are for travelers seeking unspoiled beauty and total escape. Here's an honest breakdown of each area to help you choose.

neighborhoods.name: White Sand Beach (Hat Sai Khao), Koh Chang
neighborhoods.description: Koh Chang's most developed beach and the best base for first-time visitors. The widest stretch of sand on the island is backed by a road lined with resorts, restaurants, minimarts, massage shops, ATMs, and tour operators. This is where you'll find the most nightlife (modest by Thai standards), the best restaurant selection, and the easiest access to island tours and activities. The beach itself is beautiful — wide, swimmable, and backed by forested hills. The trade-off: it's the most touristy area on Koh Chang.
highlights: Widest beach and most facilities on Koh Chang
Best restaurant and nightlife selection on the island
ATMs, 7-Elevens, pharmacies, and tour operators within walking distance
KC Grande Resort (5-star) and Kacha Resort dominate the beachfront
Easy access to songthaews connecting to other beaches
transportNotes: Tour operators arrange all island activities from here
30 minutes by songthaew to the ferry pier

neighborhoods.name: Klong Prao & Kai Bae, Koh Chang
neighborhoods.description: The middle section of Koh Chang's west coast offers the best balance of beach quality and peaceful atmosphere. Klong Prao is the island's longest beach — a wide stretch of sand backed by a river estuary and tropical gardens, with far fewer people than White Sand Beach. Kai Bae, just south, has a more intimate village feel with local restaurants and a viewpoint overlooking offshore islands. Both areas have enough facilities (restaurants, minimarts, massage shops) without feeling overdeveloped. The top resorts — Santhiya Tree and Centara Tropicana — are here.
highlights: Klong Prao — longest beach on Koh Chang with fewer crowds
Kai Bae viewpoint — stunning sunset views over the islands
Home to Santhiya Tree (9.2/10) and Centara Tropicana resorts
Quieter than White Sand Beach but still has restaurants and shops
Near Klong Plu Waterfall — walkable from some Klong Prao hotels
transportNotes: Songthaews to White Sand Beach 50 THB, to Bang Bao pier 100 THB
Scooter rental recommended for exploring beyond the immediate beach area
Some resorts arrange shuttle service to other beaches

neighborhoods.name: Lonely Beach (Hat Tha Nam), Koh Chang
neighborhoods.description: Koh Chang's backpacker hub delivers cheap bungalows, beach bars, and the island's best party atmosphere — though 'party' here means reggae bars and fire shows, not Koh Phangan-level raves. The beach is smaller and rockier than White Sand Beach but less crowded and arguably more beautiful. Budget accommodation clusters on the hillside above the beach and along the road. A couple of upscale options like Nature Beach Resort have appeared but the vibe remains laid-back and budget-friendly.
highlights: Cheapest accommodation on Koh Chang from 500 THB/night
Best beach bar scene and casual nightlife on the island
Smaller, less crowded beach than the north coast
Nature Beach Resort offers beachfront comfort at mid-range prices
Close to Koh Chang's southern attractions (Bang Bao, waterfalls)
transportNotes: Songthaews to White Sand Beach 100 THB but service is infrequent
Scooter rental essential for day trips to northern beaches
Closer to Bang Bao pier for boat trips to Koh Mak and Koh Kood

neighborhoods.name: Trat Town
neighborhoods.description: Most travelers skip Trat Town entirely, rushing to the ferry — but an overnight stay rewards you with one of eastern Thailand's most authentic small-town experiences. The nightly food market serves incredible seafood at local prices. The old town's wooden shophouses and narrow riverside lanes have genuine historic charm. Hotels are few but characterful — converted shophouses with antique furniture at budget prices. Trat Town is ideal as a first or last night before/after the islands, especially if your ferry timing doesn't align.
neighborhoods.bestFor: Transit travelers, culture seekers & budget travelers
highlights: Nightly food market with seafood at genuine Thai prices (30-80 THB)
Historic old town with century-old wooden shophouses
Trat 101 Hotel — boutique charm with 100-year-old floors at budget prices
Gem-trading heritage with small jewelry shops still operating
Easy morning transport to ferry piers for island departure
transportNotes: Songthaews and minivans to ferry piers (30-60 min, 60-100 THB)
Bus terminal has connections to Bangkok (5-6 hrs, 250-350 THB)
Trat airport (TDX) is 30 minutes from town

neighborhoods.name: Koh Mak
neighborhoods.description: A small, flat, car-free island that's the opposite of Koh Chang in every way — no 7-Elevens, no nightlife, no crowds. Just coconut palms, rubber plantations, and quiet beaches connected by flat roads perfect for cycling. Accommodation is limited to a handful of small resorts and guesthouses, mostly on the west coast facing spectacular sunsets. Koh Mak is for travelers who want genuine disconnection without Koh Kood's premium price tag. The island shuts down almost entirely in monsoon season (June-October).
neighborhoods.bestFor: Couples, nature lovers & digital detox seekers
highlights: Car-free island — explore by bicycle in under an hour
Ao Suan Yai beach has the best sunsets in the archipelago
Low Carbon Destination — eco-conscious tourism at its best
Genuine peace and quiet — no nightclubs, no jet skis, no crowds
Good snorkeling off the southern coast directly from the beach
transportNotes: Boonsiri Ferry from Koh Chang (Bang Bao) 1 hour, 450-750 THB
Speedboat from Laem Ngop pier on the mainland 1 hour
Bicycles are the main transport — most resorts provide or rent them cheaply

neighborhoods.name: Koh Kood (Ko Kut)
neighborhoods.description: Thailand's most unspoiled large island is the ultimate escape in the Trat archipelago. Crystal-clear water, pristine white-sand beaches, jungle waterfalls, and a fraction of Koh Chang's visitors. Accommodation ranges from basic bungalows (1,000-1,800 THB) to the ultra-luxurious Soneva Kiri (35,000+ THB/night). There are no ATMs, limited phone signal, and no nightlife beyond a drink at your resort. Most properties close entirely during monsoon season (June-October). Koh Kood is for travelers who want raw island beauty and are willing to trade convenience for paradise.
highlights: Pristine beaches with crystal-clear water — Thailand's Maldives equivalent
Soneva Kiri — one of Thailand's top 3 luxury resorts with Treepod Dining
Klong Chao Waterfall with natural swimming pool in the jungle
Genuine disconnection — no ATMs, limited signal, minimal development
Far fewer visitors than Koh Chang or any Andaman coast island
transportNotes: Boonsiri Ferry from Koh Chang (Bang Bao) 1.75 hours, 750-1,200 THB
Speedboat from Laem Sok pier 1.5 hours, 600-1,200 THB
Scooter rental (300 THB/day) is the only practical island transport — no public transport exists
